reference: My husband just had an examination for life insurance. Can you tell me what this means: Chris' recent examination said: "Elevated liver functions - Gamma-glutamyl transferase: thirty-seven, Serum Glutamate Oxaloacetate Transaminase: twenty-four, Serum Glutamate Pyruvate Transaminase: sixty-one, Elevated cholesterol ratio - Cholesterol ratio: four point nine seven six." Do you know what the ratios are supposed to be?
text: My husband just had an examination for life insurance. Can you tell me what this means? Chris's recent examination said elevated liver functions, gamma-glutamyl transferase 37, serum glutamate oxaloacetate transaminase 24, serum glutamate pyruvate transaminase 61, elevated cholesterol ratio 4.976. Do you know what the ratios are supposed to be?
